media.h File Reference

Public API for the Media Processing Library. More...

#include <stdbool.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>
#include "media-c/buffer.h"
#include "media-c/capabilities.h"

 Set desired capabilities on a stream.

 Retrieve the list of controls supported by the media instance.

 Read the current value of a control.

 Retrieve element metadata (name, description, version, …).

 Query compatible capabilities in the current configuration.

 Query memory allocation constraints for a stream's input buffers.

 Print a human-readable dump of the media element.
 

Detailed Description

Author
IMD Software Team
Attention

Copyright (c) 2026 STMicroelectronics. All rights reserved.

This software is licensed under terms that can be found in the LICENSE file in the root directory of this software component. If no LICENSE file comes with this software, it is provided AS-IS.

This header defines the public interface for the Media Processing Library, including functions to manage the lifecycle, streams, controls, and capabilities of a media element.

Lifecycle Management

media_get_state — Query the current lifecycle state.

media_initialize — Transition from MEDIA_STATE_NULL to MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED. Acquires hardware handles and internal resources.

media_prepare — Transition from MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED to MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED. Validates and applies static controls, allocates resources that depend on the static configuration.

media_finalize — Transition from MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED back to MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED. Releases resources allocated during preparation.

media_release — Transition from MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED back to MEDIA_STATE_NULL. Frees all resources acquired during initialization.

Information & Inspection

media_about — Retrieve element metadata (name, description, version, license, URL, open-source dependencies).

media_get_version — Get the implemented media API version (major, minor, patch).

media_inspect — Print a human-readable dump of the element (about, streams, controls) via a printf-like callback.

Streams & Capabilities

media_get_streams — List all input/output streams.

media_get_stream_capabilities — Get the current capabilities of a named stream.

media_set_stream_capabilities — Set desired capabilities on a named stream.

media_query_stream_capabilities — Query capabilities compatible with the current configuration context.

media_query_memory_allocation — Query memory alignment constraints for a stream's input buffers.

Controls

media_get_controls — List all available controls (static and dynamic).

media_get_control — Read the current value of a control by name. Can be called at any time.

media_set_control — Write a new value to a control by name. Static controls can only be set before media_prepare; dynamic controls can be changed at any time.

Function Documentation

◆ media_about()

static int media_about ( const media_t * self,
const properties_t ** properties )
inlinestatic

Returns the properties of the media implementation such as:

  • name — Name of the implementation
  • description — Human-readable description
  • version — Implementation version string
  • license — License description
  • url — Project URL
  • opensource — Open-source dependency list
Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Ownership of the returned properties is not transferred to the caller; do not free them.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[out]propertiesPointer to receive the properties_t.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or properties is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.

◆ media_finalize()

static int media_finalize ( media_t * self)
inlinestatic

This is the reverse of media_prepare(). It tears down everything that was set up during preparation:

  • Compiled GPU kernels and programs
  • Look-up tables, calibration data
  • Any resource allocated based on static control values

After this call, static controls can be changed again and the instance can be re-prepared with a subsequent call to media_prepare().

Parameters
[in,out]selfPointer to the media instance.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED.
Postcondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED on success; unchanged on failure.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEInstance is not in MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
Note
This function may block while the implementation releases GPU resources or waits for pending operations to complete.
See also
media_prepare(), media_release()

◆ media_get_control()

static int media_get_control ( const media_t * self,
const char *const name,
value_t * value )
inlinestatic

Controls can be read as soon as a media instance is available, regardless of the lifecycle state.

Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[in]nameName of the control (must match a name from media_get_controls()).
[out]valuePointer to a value_t that receives the current control value.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or value is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_NOT_FOUNDNo control with name name exists.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_set_control(), media_get_controls()

◆ media_get_controls()

static int media_get_controls ( const media_t * self,
const controls_t ** controls )
inlinestatic

Populates controls with all controls available. Controls may be static (cannot be changed after media_prepare()) or dynamic (can be changed at any time).

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Ownership of the returned list is not transferred to the caller; do not free it.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[out]controlsPointer to receive the controls_t list.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or controls is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_get_control(), media_set_control()

◆ media_get_state()

static int media_get_state ( const media_t * self,
media_state_t * state )
inlinestatic

Query the current lifecycle state.

Returns the current state of the media element. This function is stateless and can be called at any time.

Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[out]statePointer to receive the current media_state_t value.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or state is NULL.
See also
media_initialize(), media_prepare(), media_finalize(), media_release()

◆ media_get_stream_capabilities()

static int media_get_stream_capabilities ( const media_t * self,
const char *const name,
const capabilities_t ** caps )
inlinestatic

Queries the capabilities associated with the given stream name. This is a stateful function that returns the capabilities currently configured for the stream.

Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[in]nameName of the stream (must match a name from media_get_streams()).
[out]capsPointer to receive the capabilities_t. Ownership is not transferred to the caller.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or caps is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_set_stream_capabilities(), media_get_streams()

◆ media_get_streams()

static int media_get_streams ( const media_t * self,
const streams_t ** streams )
inlinestatic

Streams are the input and output interfaces of the media element. Each stream has a name, direction (input or output), and capabilities.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Ownership of the returned list is not transferred to the caller; do not free it.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[out]streamsPointer to receive the streams_t list.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or streams is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_get_stream_capabilities(), media_set_stream_capabilities()

◆ media_get_version()

static void media_get_version ( const media_t * self,
uint32_t * major,
uint32_t * minor,
uint32_t * patch )
inlinestatic
Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[out]majorPointer to receive the major version number.
[out]minorPointer to receive the minor version number.
[out]patchPointer to receive the patch version number.

◆ media_initialize()

static int media_initialize ( media_t * self)
inlinestatic

This is the first mandatory step of the media lifecycle. It acquires the resources needed for the media element to operate:

  • Hardware handles (sensor, accelerator, DMA channel, …)
  • Internal bookkeeping structures
Note
Processing framework contexts (OpenCL, CUDA, CPU backend, …) are set up by the implementation's constructor so that controls can query hardware capabilities before this call.
Parameters
[in,out]selfPointer to the media instance.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_NULL.
Postcondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED on success; unchanged on failure.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEInstance is not in MEDIA_STATE_NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDOne or more virtual functions are not set.
See also
media_release(), media_prepare()

◆ media_inspect()

static void media_inspect ( const media_t * self,
int(* print_func )(const char *,...) )
inlinestatic

Writes a summary of the media instance to the provided print function, including the about properties, stream list, and control list.

Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[in]print_funcPrintf-like callback used for output.
See also
media_about(), media_get_streams(), media_get_controls()

◆ media_prepare()

static int media_prepare ( media_t * self)
inlinestatic

During this step the implementation:

  1. Validates every static control — some are mandatory and the call fails if they have not been set or hold invalid values.
  2. Applies the static control values (e.g. selects an OpenCL device, configures a processing mode, sets sensor registers).
  3. Allocates additional resources that depend on the static configuration (kernel programs, look-up tables, calibration data, …).

All static controls must be set via media_set_control() before calling this function. Dynamic controls can still be changed after preparation.

Parameters
[in,out]selfPointer to the media instance.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED.
All mandatory static controls have been set with valid values.
Postcondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED on success; unchanged on failure.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEInstance is not in MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
Note
This function may block while the implementation compiles GPU kernels, loads calibration files, or performs other heavyweight preparation.
See also
media_finalize(), media_set_control()

◆ media_query_memory_allocation()

static int media_query_memory_allocation ( const media_t * self,
const char * name,
properties_t ** constraints )
inlinestatic

Retrieves the memory allocation constraints required for optimal performance with the specified stream. Applications can use this information to allocate properly aligned buffers, avoiding runtime memory copies.

Constraint types supported:

  • memory-alignment — Base memory address alignment (bytes)
  • stride-alignment — Row stride alignment (bytes)
  • size-alignment — Buffer size granularity (bytes)
  • plane-alignment — Plane offset alignment (bytes)
Note
This function can be called after the instance is initialized.
In some systems like OpenCL, constraints are device-specific and can only be queried after the device has been selected by a control.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[in]nameName of the stream.
[out]constraintsPointer to receive the properties_t describing the constraints.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or constraints is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_get_streams()

◆ media_query_stream_capabilities()

static int media_query_stream_capabilities ( const media_t * self,
const char * name,
capabilities_t ** caps )
inlinestatic

Determines the compatible capabilities of a stream taking into account the current configuration and dependencies between streams:

  • For input streams, lists all available capabilities.
  • For output streams, returns only the capabilities compatible with the current configuration of its dependent input streams.
Note
This function is stateless and can be called at any time.
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[in]nameName of the stream (must match a name from media_get_streams()).
[out]capsPointer to receive the capabilities_t.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or caps is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_get_stream_capabilities(), media_set_stream_capabilities()

◆ media_release()

static int media_release ( media_t * self)
inlinestatic

Releases every resource that was set up during media_initialize():

  • Hardware handles and device connections
  • Allocated buffers and internal structures

After this call the instance returns to its initial state and can be re-initialized with a new call to media_initialize().

Parameters
[in,out]selfPointer to the media instance.
Precondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED (media_finalize() must have been called first if the instance was previously prepared).
Postcondition
State is MEDIA_STATE_NULL on success; unchanged on failure.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEInstance is not in MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_initialize(), media_finalize()

◆ media_set_control()

static int media_set_control ( const media_t * self,
const char * name,
const value_t value )
inlinestatic

Controls can be set as soon as a media instance is available. Controls come in two flavours:

  • Static controls (CTRL_FLAGS_STATIC) — can be set at any time before media_prepare() is called (i.e. while in MEDIA_STATE_NULL or MEDIA_STATE_INITIALIZED). Attempting to change a static control once the instance is in MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED or later returns MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATE. Some static controls are mandatory: media_prepare() will fail if they have not been set to a valid value.
  • Dynamic controls (CTRL_FLAGS_DYNAMIC) — can be changed at any time, regardless of the current state (e.g. gain, exposure, threshold).
Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[in]nameName of the control (must match a name from media_get_controls()).
[in]valueThe value_t to assign.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself or name is NULL, or value has an invalid type.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_STATEname is a static control and the instance is already in MEDIA_STATE_PREPARED or later.
MEDIA_ERROR_NOT_FOUNDNo control with name name exists.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_get_control(), media_get_controls(), media_prepare()

◆ media_set_stream_capabilities()

static int media_set_stream_capabilities ( const media_t * self,
const char *const name,
const capabilities_t * caps )
inlinestatic

Configures the capabilities for the specified stream. The implementation validates that the requested capabilities are compatible with the current configuration.

Parameters
[in]selfPointer to the media instance.
[in]nameName of the stream (must match a name from media_get_streams()).
[in]capsPointer to the capabilities_t to apply.
Return values
MEDIA_ERROR_NONESuccess.
MEDIA_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETERself, name, or caps is NULL.
MEDIA_ERROR_UNIMPLEMENTEDVirtual function not set.
See also
media_get_stream_capabilities(), media_query_stream_capabilities()
